template.frontend-base.title: AI-RULES Frontend Base Template
template.frontend-base.desc.1: This is a frontend baseline rule set for React/Vue/Svelte style projects.
template.frontend-base.desc.2: Framework templates can extend this base and add framework-specific rules.
template.frontend-base.desc.3: Patterns in detect are illustrative and can be enhanced by AST/semantic analysis in CLI.
template.react-ts.title: AI-RULES React + TypeScript Template
template.react-ts.desc.1: This template extends the frontend base with React-specific rules.
template.react-ts.desc.2: Rules use the RULE block syntax for deterministic parsing and audit prompt generation.
template.react-ts.desc.3: Patterns in detect are illustrative and can be strengthened with ast-grep/semantic checks.
template.vue-ts.title: AI-RULES Vue + TypeScript Template
template.vue-ts.desc.1: This template extends frontend-base with Vue-specific rules.
template.vue-ts.desc.2: It covers computed purity, list key stability, and DOM access constraints.
template.vue-ts.desc.3: Patterns in detect are illustrative and can be enhanced by AST/semantic analysis in CLI.
rule.ARCH-101.intent: UI components must not initiate network requests directly.
rule.ARCH-101.fix: Move request logic into the service layer and call services from UI components.
rule.ARCH-101.prompt.violation: Detected direct network request calls in UI layer.
rule.ARCH-101.prompt.requirement: UI components must not contain request logic.
rule.ARCH-101.prompt.solution: Move requests to services and reuse the existing API client.
rule.ARCH-102.intent: UI components must not access storage layer or databases directly.
rule.ARCH-102.fix: Move data access to server/service layer and expose through API boundaries.
rule.ARCH-102.prompt.violation: Detected data-layer or database access in UI layer.
rule.ARCH-102.prompt.requirement: UI layer must not depend on DB or ORM.
rule.ARCH-102.prompt.solution: Refactor access through service and API boundaries.
rule.ARCH-103.intent: Disallow reverse cross-layer dependencies (ui -> service -> data).
rule.ARCH-103.fix: Expose capabilities to UI via service abstractions.
rule.ARCH-103.prompt.requirement: Dependency direction must follow layering architecture.
rule.ARCH-103.prompt.solution: Refactor module boundaries and expose only service contracts.
rule.UI-201.intent: Prefer project UI library components over native HTML controls.
rule.UI-201.fix: Replace native tags with project UI components.
rule.UI-201.prompt.requirement: Use unified project UI components whenever possible.
rule.UI-201.prompt.solution: Replace with design-system components for consistency.
rule.UI-202.intent: Form controls must include accessibility labels.
rule.UI-202.fix: Add aria labels or bind labels properly.
rule.UI-202.prompt.violation: Detected form controls missing accessibility semantics.
rule.UI-202.prompt.solution: Add aria-label/aria-labelledby or explicit label linkage.
rule.TS-401.fix: Replace any with explicit and safe types.
rule.TS-401.prompt.solution: Add concrete types for variables, params, and returns.
rule.STYLE-501.intent: Component names must be PascalCase and aligned with file names.
rule.STYLE-501.fix: Rename component to PascalCase and align file name.
rule.STYLE-501.prompt.requirement: Component and file naming should follow conventions.
rule.STYLE-501.prompt.solution: Normalize naming to PascalCase and update file names.
rule.STYLE-502.intent: Avoid large inline functions inside components.
rule.STYLE-502.fix: Extract logic into standalone functions or custom hooks.
rule.STYLE-502.prompt.violation: Detected oversized inline function in component.
rule.STYLE-502.prompt.requirement: Components should remain focused and maintainable.
rule.STYLE-502.prompt.solution: Move complex logic to hooks or utility modules.
rule.REACT-301.intent: Do not run side effects during render.
rule.REACT-301.fix: Move side effects to useEffect or event handlers.
rule.REACT-301.prompt.violation: Detected side effects during render phase.
rule.REACT-301.prompt.solution: Relocate side effects to lifecycle-safe locations.
rule.REACT-302.intent: Hooks can only be called in components or custom hooks.
rule.REACT-302.fix: Move hook invocations into component top-level or custom hooks.
rule.REACT-302.prompt.requirement: Hook call rules must be respected.
rule.REACT-302.prompt.solution: Refactor hook usage to legal call sites.
rule.VUE-301.intent: Computed functions must remain pure and free of side effects.
rule.VUE-301.fix: Move side effects into watch or onMounted.
rule.VUE-302.intent: v-for must provide a stable key.
rule.VUE-302.prompt.requirement: List rendering must include stable keys.
rule.VUE-302.prompt.solution: Provide a unique key for each item.
rule.VUE-303.intent: Avoid direct DOM manipulation inside components.
rule.VUE-303.fix: Use refs or Vue-provided APIs for DOM access.
rule.VUE-303.prompt.requirement: DOM operations should be mediated by Vue.
rule.VUE-303.prompt.solution: Replace direct DOM access with ref/Vue APIs.
prompt.audit.system: You are a strict architecture auditor. Use resolved rule content to audit layering, typing, security, and maintainability across the project's stack.
prompt.audit.user: Read .ai-rules.md and rules-config.json, then audit the target code and output violations, locations, and actionable fixes.
prompt.repair.system: You are a code repair assistant. Follow resolved rule constraints and produce executable minimal-change fixes.
prompt.repair.user: Based on violated rules and project context assets, generate a minimal patch plan and include patch-ready edits when helpful.
prompt.audit.cli: Start a semantic audit: read .ai-rules rules and configuration in the project, check code against architectural intent, and output ai-rule-report.json. Return strict JSON with this shape: { "version": "1.0", "violations": [ { "issueId": "ISSUE-001", "ruleId": "ARCH-101", "severity": "FATAL|WARN|INFO", "file": "src/...", "line": 1, "description": "...", "fixSuggestion": "...", "repairPrompt": "..." } ] }. Each violation must include a unique issueId and a repairPrompt.
template.c-cpp.title: AI-RULES C/C++ Template
template.c-cpp.desc.1: This template governs architecture, safety, concurrency, and build quality for C/C++ projects.
template.c-cpp.desc.2: Rules use the RULE block syntax with content keys separated from structure.
template.c-cpp.desc.3: Patterns in detect are illustrative and can be enhanced by AST/semantic analysis in CLI.
rule.CPP-ARCH-101.intent: Application and domain layers must not call platform-specific APIs directly.
rule.CPP-ARCH-101.fix: Wrap platform capabilities behind adapter interfaces and depend on abstractions.
rule.CPP-ARCH-101.prompt.violation: Detected direct dependency on platform implementation from upper layers.
rule.CPP-ARCH-101.prompt.requirement: Platform capabilities must be isolated via adapters.
rule.CPP-ARCH-101.prompt.solution: Introduce adapter abstractions and replace direct platform calls.
rule.CPP-ARCH-102.intent: Disallow reverse layering dependencies (e.g., domain depending on infra details).
rule.CPP-ARCH-102.fix: Apply dependency inversion and converge upper layers to interfaces.
rule.CPP-ARCH-102.prompt.solution: Move concrete details down to infra and depend on contracts.
rule.CPP-MEM-201.intent: Disallow raw new/delete; prefer RAII and smart pointers.
rule.CPP-MEM-201.fix: Replace raw ownership with unique_ptr/shared_ptr or stack allocation.
rule.CPP-MEM-201.prompt.requirement: Resource lifetime must be managed via RAII.
rule.CPP-MEM-201.prompt.solution: Refactor ownership using smart pointers and explicit lifetime semantics.
rule.CPP-MEM-202.intent: Do not return dangling pointers or references.
rule.CPP-MEM-202.fix: Use value semantics, smart pointers, or extend object lifetime safely.
rule.CPP-MEM-202.prompt.requirement: Returned objects must have safe lifetime guarantees.
rule.CPP-MEM-202.prompt.solution: Adjust return strategy and repair lifetime boundaries.
rule.CPP-MEM-203.intent: File/socket/lock handles must be wrapped by RAII types.
rule.CPP-MEM-203.fix: Introduce resource wrappers with deterministic release in destructors.
rule.CPP-MEM-203.prompt.violation: Detected unmanaged resource handles without RAII.
rule.CPP-MEM-203.prompt.requirement: Resource release must be automatic and predictable.
rule.CPP-MEM-203.prompt.solution: Create guard/wrapper types and remove manual release patterns.
rule.CPP-CONC-301.intent: Shared mutable state access must use synchronization strategy.
rule.CPP-CONC-301.fix: Protect shared state with mutex/atomic or lock-free safe structures.
rule.CPP-CONC-301.prompt.violation: Detected unsynchronized access to shared mutable state.
rule.CPP-CONC-301.prompt.requirement: Concurrent read/write operations must be thread-safe.
rule.CPP-CONC-301.prompt.solution: Add synchronization primitives and validate memory visibility.
rule.CPP-CONC-302.intent: Do not run blocking I/O while holding locks.
rule.CPP-CONC-302.fix: Reduce lock scope or move blocking I/O outside critical sections.
rule.CPP-CONC-302.prompt.violation: Detected blocking I/O inside lock scope.
rule.CPP-CONC-302.prompt.requirement: Critical sections should stay minimal and non-blocking.
rule.CPP-CONC-302.prompt.solution: Refactor critical section boundaries and reduce lock contention.
rule.CPP-API-401.intent: Function error paths must be explicit.
rule.CPP-API-401.fix: Standardize failures using error codes, exceptions, or expected-like types.
rule.CPP-API-401.prompt.violation: Detected implicit or unclear error path handling.
rule.CPP-API-401.prompt.requirement: Callers must be able to detect and handle failures.
rule.CPP-API-401.prompt.solution: Define a consistent error contract and update call sites.
rule.CPP-API-402.intent: Public APIs should enforce const correctness and noexcept policy.
rule.CPP-API-402.fix: Add const to non-mutating methods and define explicit noexcept semantics.
rule.CPP-API-402.prompt.requirement: API contracts should be stable and exception behavior predictable.
rule.CPP-API-402.prompt.solution: Complete const/noexcept declarations and align implementation.
rule.CPP-BUILD-501.intent: Build must enable strict warnings and treat them as errors.
rule.CPP-BUILD-501.prompt.requirement: Compiler diagnostics must be enforced strictly.
rule.CPP-BUILD-501.prompt.solution: Add strict flags and clean existing warnings.
rule.CPP-BUILD-502.intent: All targets must use a unified C++ standard version.
rule.CPP-BUILD-502.fix: Set and enforce one C++ standard for all targets.
rule.CPP-BUILD-502.prompt.violation: Detected inconsistent C++ standard settings across targets.
rule.CPP-BUILD-502.prompt.requirement: Targets should share consistent standard settings to avoid ABI/behavior drift.
rule.CPP-BUILD-502.prompt.solution: Normalize standard configuration and validate full build.
rule.CPP-SEC-601.fix: Replace with bounded and safer alternatives.
rule.CPP-SEC-601.prompt.requirement: Memory and input operations must enforce bounds safety.
rule.CPP-SEC-601.prompt.solution: Use safe API replacements and add explicit length checks.
rule.CPP-SEC-602.intent: External input must be validated for length and format.
rule.CPP-SEC-602.fix: Apply boundary and format validation before processing input.
rule.CPP-SEC-602.prompt.requirement: Input data must be validated before use.
rule.CPP-SEC-602.prompt.solution: Add validation paths and reject invalid data early.
rule.CPP-TEST-701.intent: Core module changes must include unit/regression tests.
rule.CPP-TEST-701.fix: Add minimal verifiable tests for changed critical paths.
rule.CPP-TEST-701.prompt.violation: Detected core changes without corresponding tests.
rule.CPP-TEST-701.prompt.solution: Add targeted tests and verify before merge.
template.java-spring.title: AI-RULES Java + Spring Template
template.java-spring.desc.1: This template extends java-base with Spring-specific rules.
template.java-spring.desc.2: It covers controller boundaries, security, transactions, and validation constraints.
template.java-spring.desc.3: Patterns in detect are illustrative and can be enhanced by AST/semantic analysis in CLI.
template.java-base.title: AI-RULES Java Base Template
template.java-base.desc.1: This template targets general Java projects and service foundations.
template.java-base.desc.2: It covers security, DTO contracts, data access, testing, and observability.
template.java-base.desc.3: Patterns in detect are illustrative and can be enhanced by AST/semantic analysis in CLI.
rule.JAVA-ARCH-101.intent: Controllers must not depend on repositories directly.
rule.JAVA-ARCH-101.fix: Move business/data access logic into service layer.
rule.JAVA-ARCH-101.prompt.violation: Detected direct data-layer access from controller.
rule.JAVA-ARCH-101.prompt.requirement: Layering must follow Controller -> Service -> Repository.
rule.JAVA-ARCH-101.prompt.solution: Refactor data access into service and call service from controller.
rule.JAVA-ARCH-102.intent: Do not instantiate repositories or data objects in controller layer.
rule.JAVA-ARCH-102.fix: Use dependency injection and depend on service contracts.
rule.JAVA-ARCH-102.prompt.violation: Detected cross-layer object construction in controller.
rule.JAVA-ARCH-102.prompt.requirement: Controller layer should only orchestrate request/response flow.
rule.JAVA-ARCH-102.prompt.solution: Remove cross-layer dependencies and inject service abstractions.
rule.JAVA-SEC-201.intent: Disallow SQL construction via string concatenation.
rule.JAVA-SEC-201.fix: Use parameterized queries or safe ORM query APIs.
rule.JAVA-SEC-201.prompt.solution: Refactor query logic to use bound parameters.
rule.JAVA-SEC-202.intent: Disallow overly permissive CORS settings (e.g., origins=*).
rule.JAVA-SEC-202.fix: Restrict trusted origins and isolate config per environment.
rule.JAVA-SEC-202.prompt.requirement: CORS policy must follow least privilege.
rule.JAVA-SEC-202.prompt.solution: Use explicit origin allowlist and environment-specific config.
rule.JAVA-API-301.intent: RequestBody inputs should be validated with @Valid.
rule.JAVA-API-301.fix: Add validation annotations on DTOs and enforce @Valid at entry.
rule.JAVA-API-301.prompt.requirement: External input must be validated before business execution.
rule.JAVA-API-301.prompt.solution: Add DTO constraints and @Valid in controller signatures.
rule.JAVA-API-302.intent: API responses should use DTOs instead of exposing entities directly.
rule.JAVA-API-302.fix: Introduce DTO mapping and hide internal entity fields.
rule.JAVA-API-302.prompt.requirement: API contracts must stay decoupled from persistence models.
rule.JAVA-API-302.prompt.solution: Return DTOs and control serialized fields explicitly.
rule.JAVA-DATA-401.intent: Write operations must define explicit transaction boundaries.
rule.JAVA-DATA-401.fix: Add transaction semantics on write service methods.
rule.JAVA-DATA-401.prompt.violation: Detected write path without transaction semantics.
rule.JAVA-DATA-401.prompt.requirement: Consistency-sensitive operations must run in transactions.
rule.JAVA-DATA-401.prompt.solution: Add transaction annotations and verify rollback behavior.
rule.JAVA-DATA-402.intent: Disallow unbounded full-table reads in service layer.
rule.JAVA-DATA-402.fix: Use pagination and enforce default page limits.
rule.JAVA-DATA-402.prompt.requirement: List queries must be paginated and bounded.
rule.JAVA-DATA-402.prompt.solution: Introduce paging params and safe defaults.
rule.JAVA-TEST-501.intent: Core business changes must include tests.
rule.JAVA-TEST-501.fix: Add service/controller unit or integration tests.
rule.JAVA-TEST-501.prompt.violation: Detected core changes without test coverage.
rule.JAVA-TEST-501.prompt.solution: Add focused tests and validate pass status.
rule.JAVA-OBS-601.intent: Critical business paths should emit structured logs.
rule.JAVA-OBS-601.fix: Add structured logs with contextual fields and error metadata.
rule.JAVA-OBS-601.prompt.violation: Detected insufficient logs in critical path.
rule.JAVA-OBS-601.prompt.solution: Add standardized structured logging on key branches.
template.python-fastapi.title: AI-RULES Python + FastAPI Template
template.python-fastapi.desc.1: This template extends python-base with FastAPI-specific rules.
template.python-fastapi.desc.2: It covers route boundaries, input validation, and async call constraints.
template.python-fastapi.desc.3: Patterns in detect are illustrative and can be enhanced by AST/semantic analysis in CLI.
template.python-base.title: AI-RULES Python Base Template
template.python-base.desc.1: This template fits general Python scenarios (scripts, algorithms, ML/model, and foundational services).
template.python-base.desc.2: It covers code safety, typing contracts, security baseline, testing, and reproducibility.
template.python-base.desc.3: Patterns in detect are illustrative and can be enhanced by AST/semantic analysis in CLI.
rule.PYBASE-CODE-101.fix: Catch explicit exception types and log useful context.
rule.PYBASE-CODE-101.prompt.solution: Replace with explicit exception classes and add logging.
rule.PYBASE-CODE-102.fix: Use None as default and initialize inside function body.
rule.PYBASE-CODE-102.prompt.requirement: Default arguments must avoid shared mutable state.
rule.PYBASE-CODE-102.prompt.solution: Switch to None + local initialization pattern.
rule.PYBASE-TYPE-201.intent: Public functions should define parameter and return type hints.
rule.PYBASE-TYPE-201.fix: Add type hints to public APIs.
rule.PYBASE-TYPE-201.prompt.violation: Detected public function without type hints.
rule.PYBASE-TYPE-201.prompt.requirement: Public contracts should be explicit and typed.
rule.PYBASE-TYPE-202.fix: Prefer concrete types, protocols, or bounded generics.
rule.PYBASE-TYPE-202.prompt.requirement: Type system should represent real constraints.
rule.PYBASE-TYPE-202.prompt.solution: Replace Any with precise type definitions.
rule.PYBASE-SEC-301.fix: Use safe parsing alternatives (e.g., ast.literal_eval or whitelist interpreters).
rule.PYBASE-SEC-301.prompt.requirement: Untrusted input must never reach dynamic execution.
rule.PYBASE-SEC-301.prompt.solution: Replace dynamic execution and validate input rigorously.
rule.PYBASE-SEC-302.fix: Use safer serialization formats or signed payload validation.
rule.PYBASE-SEC-302.prompt.requirement: Deserialization must verify source and integrity.
rule.PYBASE-SEC-302.prompt.solution: Switch serialization strategy and add integrity checks.
rule.PYBASE-TEST-401.intent: Core module changes should include tests.
rule.PYBASE-TEST-401.fix: Add unit/regression tests for critical branches.
rule.PYBASE-TEST-401.prompt.violation: Detected core change without test coverage.
rule.PYBASE-TEST-401.prompt.solution: Add focused tests and verify passing results.
rule.PYBASE-REPRO-501.intent: Training/experiment entrypoints should control random seeds for reproducibility.
rule.PYBASE-REPRO-501.fix: Set and centralize random/numpy/torch seeds at entrypoint.
rule.PYBASE-REPRO-501.prompt.violation: Detected missing reproducibility control in experiment entrypoint.
rule.PYBASE-REPRO-501.prompt.requirement: Results should be reproducible and comparable.
rule.PYBASE-REPRO-501.prompt.solution: Add unified seed settings and record them in logs.
rule.PY-ARCH-101.intent: Route handlers must not create DB sessions or access data layer directly.
rule.PY-ARCH-101.fix: Move data access into service/repository layers.
rule.PY-ARCH-101.prompt.violation: Detected direct database access from API layer.
rule.PY-ARCH-101.prompt.requirement: Layering must follow Router -> Service -> Repository.
rule.PY-ARCH-101.prompt.solution: Refactor DB operations out of route handlers.
rule.PY-ARCH-102.intent: Route handlers should not depend on repositories directly.
rule.PY-ARCH-102.fix: Expose business capabilities through service layer.
rule.PY-ARCH-102.prompt.requirement: API layer should orchestrate IO boundaries only.
rule.PY-ARCH-102.prompt.solution: Adjust dependency direction via service abstractions.
rule.PY-SEC-202.intent: Disallow disabled JWT verification or permissive CORS defaults.
rule.PY-SEC-202.fix: Enable verification and restrict CORS to trusted origins.
rule.PY-SEC-202.prompt.requirement: Security config must follow least privilege.
rule.PY-SEC-202.prompt.solution: Harden auth and CORS configuration per environment.
rule.PY-API-301.intent: Endpoint inputs should be defined and validated by Pydantic models.
rule.PY-API-301.fix: Add schema models for body/query inputs and enforce validation.
rule.PY-API-301.prompt.requirement: Inputs must be structured and validated.
rule.PY-API-301.prompt.solution: Bind Pydantic schemas to endpoint signatures.
rule.PY-API-302.intent: Endpoints should use response_model instead of returning ORM objects directly.
rule.PY-API-302.fix: Define response schemas and map ORM to API models.
rule.PY-API-302.prompt.requirement: Response contracts must be stable and explicit.
rule.PY-API-302.prompt.solution: Add response_model and control field exposure.
rule.PY-ASYNC-401.intent: Do not use blocking HTTP clients inside async routes.
rule.PY-ASYNC-401.fix: Use async clients such as httpx.AsyncClient.
rule.PY-ASYNC-401.prompt.violation: Detected blocking I/O in async execution path.
rule.PY-ASYNC-401.prompt.requirement: Async path must avoid blocking operations.
rule.PY-ASYNC-401.prompt.solution: Replace blocking calls with async equivalents.
rule.PY-ASYNC-402.intent: Disallow time.sleep in async-capable code paths.
rule.PY-ASYNC-402.fix: Replace with asyncio.sleep or background task scheduling.
rule.PY-ASYNC-402.prompt.requirement: Event loop must remain schedulable and responsive.
rule.PY-ASYNC-402.prompt.solution: Refactor blocking waits to async-safe timing.
rule.PY-OBS-601.intent: Critical exception paths should emit structured logs.
rule.PY-OBS-601.fix: Add trace/context/error-code fields in standardized logs.
rule.PY-OBS-601.prompt.violation: Detected insufficient logging in critical flow.
rule.PY-OBS-601.prompt.solution: Add standardized structured logs on key branches.
prompt.setup.copilot: Create two reusable chat shortcuts for this repo in GitHub Copilot Chat: '/law-audit' and '/law-fix'. '/law-audit' should run: ai-law audit --locale en and then strictly return ai-rule-report.json in the required schema. '/law-fix' should ask for issueId first, then run: ai-law fix --issueId <ISSUE_ID>, and use the copied prompt to produce minimal patch-ready edits.
prompt.setup.codex: Create two reusable command prompts for Codex in this repo context: '/law-audit' and '/law-fix'. '/law-audit' executes ai-law audit --locale en and outputs ai-rule-report.json only. '/law-fix' asks for issueId, executes ai-law fix --issueId <ISSUE_ID>, and returns minimal architecture-safe code changes based on the copied prompt.
prompt.setup.cursor: Set up two reusable prompts in Cursor for this repo: 'law-audit' and 'law-fix'. 'law-audit' should run ai-law audit --locale en and output ai-rule-report.json strictly in JSON schema. 'law-fix' should ask for issueId, run ai-law fix --issueId <ISSUE_ID>, and then generate minimal patch-ready edits.
prompt.setup.claude-code: Set up two reusable prompts for Claude Code in this repo: 'law-audit' and 'law-fix'. 'law-audit' should run ai-law audit --locale en and produce ai-rule-report.json in strict JSON schema. 'law-fix' should request issueId, run ai-law fix --issueId <ISSUE_ID>, and produce minimal, architecture-preserving patch suggestions.
prompt.setup.custom: Configure your AI coding tool with two reusable actions in this repository: 'law-audit' and 'law-fix'. 'law-audit': run ai-law audit --locale en and output only ai-rule-report.json in the required schema. 'law-fix': ask for issueId, run ai-law fix --issueId <ISSUE_ID>, then generate the smallest safe patch from the copied prompt.
prompt.setup.noSlashHint: If this tool does not support real slash commands, save these as reusable prompt templates, snippets, or quick actions.
